darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Mitochondrial protein import in S. cerevisiae is a sophisticated multi-step process that delivers nuclear-encoded proteins to their correct mitochondrial compartments. Most mitochondrial proteins are synthesized in the cytosol with N-terminal presequences and must cross one or both mitochondrial membranes. The process involves multiple translocase complexes: TOM (outer membrane), TIM23 and TIM22 (inner membrane), SAM (outer membrane β-barrel proteins), and MIA (intermembrane space). Import is driven by the membrane potential, ATP hydrolysis by mtHsp70, and coordinated action of chaperones. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from cytosolic precursor recognition through membrane translocation, presequence cleavage, and protein folding in target compartments.

graph TD
    A[Nuclear Gene Expression] --> B[Cytosolic Translation]
    B --> C[Mitochondrial Precursor Protein]
    C --> D{Targeting Signal?}
    
    D -->|N-terminal Presequence| E[Matrix-Destined Protein]
    D -->|Internal Signal| F[Inner Membrane Protein]
    D -->|β-barrel Signal| G[Outer Membrane Protein]
    D -->|Cysteine Motifs| H[IMS Protein]
    
    E --> I[Cytosolic Chaperones]
    I --> J[Hsp70 Hsp90 System]
    J --> K[Unfolded Precursor]
    
    K --> L{TOM Complex Recognition?}
    L -->|Tom20 Receptor| M[Presequence Binding]
    L -->|Tom70 Receptor| N[Internal Signal Binding]
    
    M --> O[Tom22 Central Receptor]
    N --> O
    O --> P{TOM Channel Translocation?}
    
    P -->|Tom40 Pore| Q[Outer Membrane Crossing]
    Q --> R[Protein in IMS]
    
    R --> S{Destination Decision?}
    S -->|Matrix| T[TIM23 Complex]
    S -->|Inner Membrane| U[TIM22 Complex]
    S -->|IMS| V[MIA Pathway]
    S -->|Outer Membrane| W[SAM Complex]
    
    T --> X{TIM23 Components?}
    X -->|Tim23 Tim17| Y[Inner Membrane Channel]
    X -->|Tim50| Z[IMS Receptor]
    X -->|Tim21| AA[Respiratory Chain Link]
    
    Y --> AB{Membrane Potential Required?}
    AB -->|Δψ Present| AC[Electrophoretic Driving Force]
    AB -->|No Δψ| AD[Import Stalled]
    
    AC --> AE[Presequence Insertion]
    AE --> AF{PAM Complex Engagement?}
    
    AF -->|Tim44 Platform| AG[mtHsp70 Recruitment]
    AG --> AH[ATP-Dependent Pulling]
    AH --> AI[Complete Translocation]
    
    AI --> AJ[MPP Matrix Processing Peptidase]
    AJ --> AK[Presequence Cleavage]
    AK --> AL[Mature Matrix Protein]
    
    AL --> AM[mtHsp70 Folding]
    AM --> AN{Requires Hsp60?}
    AN -->|Yes| AO[Hsp60-Hsp10 Chaperonin]
    AN -->|No| AP[Spontaneous Folding]
    
    AO --> AQ[Functional Matrix Protein]
    AP --> AQ
    
    U --> AR{TIM22 Pathway?}
    AR -->|Tim22 Tim54 Tim18| AS[Carrier Protein Import]
    AS --> AT[Tim9-Tim10 Chaperones]
    AT --> AU[Lateral Release into IM]
    AU --> AV[Functional Carrier]
    
    V --> AW{MIA Pathway Components?}
    AW -->|Mia40 Oxidoreductase| AX[Disulfide Bond Formation]
    AW -->|Erv1 Sulfhydryl Oxidase| AY[Electron Transfer]
    
    AX --> AZ[Oxidized IMS Protein]
    AY --> AX
    AZ --> BA[Trapped in IMS]
    
    W --> BB{SAM Complex Assembly?}
    BB -->|Sam50 Core| BC[β-barrel Insertion]
    BB -->|Sam35 Sam37| BD[Substrate Recognition]
    
    BC --> BE[Outer Membrane Integration]
    BE --> BF{Additional Processing?}
    BF -->|Mdm10 ERMES| BG[ER-Mitochondria Contacts]
    BF -->|Complete| BH[Functional OM Protein]
    
    F --> BI{Hydrophobic Protein Path?}
    BI -->|Stop-Transfer| BJ[TIM23 Lateral Release]
    BI -->|Carrier| U
    
    BJ --> BK[Inner Membrane Insertion]
    BK --> BL[Topology Establishment]
    
    AQ --> BM{Protein Complex Assembly?}
    AV --> BM
    BA --> BM
    BH --> BM
    BL --> BM
    
    BM -->|Respiratory Complexes| BN[Complex I-V Assembly]
    BM -->|Metabolic Enzymes| BO[TCA Cycle Enzymes]
    BM -->|Transporters| BP[Metabolite Carriers]
    
    BN --> BQ[Functional Mitochondria]
    BO --> BQ
    BP --> BQ
    
    BQ --> BR{Quality Control?}
    BR -->|Misfolded| BS[AAA Proteases]
    BR -->|Damaged| BT(\1)
    BR -->|Functional| BU[Cellular Respiration]
    
    BS --> BV[/Protein Degradation/]
    BT --> BW[Organelle Turnover]
    BU --> BX[ATP Production]

name: Yeast Mitochondrial Protein Import
notes: Created based on comprehensive research on S. cerevisiae mitochondrial protein import, the paradigm system for understanding organellar protein targeting.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive peer-reviewed literature on yeast mitochondrial protein import, including structural and functional studies of translocase complexes, targeting signals, and energy requirements. The pathway components, translocation mechanisms, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. S. cerevisiae serves as the primary model for understanding mitochondrial biogenesis in all eukaryotes.

sources.title: Mitochondrial protein import: from proteomics to functional mechanisms
The protein import machinery of mitochondria-a regulatory hub in metabolism, stress, and disease

verifiedBy: Manual creation with PubMed literature review
